reviews.review_title: good fun!  A small thing that annoys me
reviews.review_description:
Once I lose the endless mode, it instantly goes back to the "lobby" screen and I can't see how many points I made. Once the game has been lost, I think it would make sense to have to confirm to go back to the menu or at least find a solution on how the player could see his latest score. 
If you fix this, I will rate it 5 stars.

developer_response.body: Thanks for leaving your feedback! As for the issue you've mentioned, we will be looking at ways to improve this part as we understand players knowing their Endless scores is pretty important :)

reviews.review_description:
A puzzle game with cardio... I hope there was more of this genre!

One little issue I've found is with the "calibration" I'd like to adjust the height of the screen and the height of the punching device separately. 
For me screen is a little to high but if I lower it then the punching device is too low!
Please devs consider adding that for more friendly calibration.

I hope game is successful enough to add more content to it like cosmetics.

I'm having fun so far playing with my wife.
Please make the online server to last ok? 😉

developer_response.body: We do need more games like this one, that's why we created one! As for the calibration, we will see if we can do anything about it, but that's how we designed the game to work so it may be tricky to change, but stay tuned. Thanks for your review!

reviews.review_description:
An unusual combination of a boxing game and several puzzle games like Tetris, Match 4, and Puyo...

Great multiplayer fun for up to 9 players, pure competition, and loads of laughs.

developer_response.body: We are glad you are enjoying this unique combination of genres :) Thanks a lot for leaving your review, it means a lot!

reviews.review_description:
Great fun, Tetris on steroids, punch your way to success, top the leaderboard and become king of the ring…..Tetris .
Be prepared for aching muscles after a few rounds.
Great idea and works really well.
Well done devs.

developer_response.body: Yeah, those punches are real and your muscles can feel that! Thanks for the positive review :)

reviews.review_description:
Usually, puzzle games require quick thinking, but not this one.
You’ll need explosive punching power and the stamina to outlast every other player. lol

developer_response.body: And it's a real intensive workout as well! Thank you for the review :)

reviews.review_description: With the new style, you can learn the moves easily. However, it's not an easy game because you combine punches while considering rotation and movement to match. To train the body and brain at the same time. Merci Majerik!
Combining Tetris with motion-sensing fitness is really interesting.

developer_response.body: Thank you for appreciating our idea, we are really proud of it as well :)

reviews.review_description: Retro but different take on Tetris and Match 4 games. Graphics were super clean and took me back to the 80’s when neon and video games went mainstream. Loved the look of the game and the game room. I only played single player and thought the pacing was a little slow. Couldn’t figure out how to adjust it so that it moved faster (if you can).

developer_response.body: We are glad that you enjoyed the visuals so much - we tried our best to emulate those 80s retro vibes the best as we could so it's very rewarding to see people ''vibing'' with it :) As for the pacing, we will investigate and see what can be done. Thanks for your review!

reviews.review_description: It’s a really great game that grabs your attention from the very first moments. It has a very interesting concept that makes the gameplay feel fresh, enjoyable, and highly satisfying. Another big advantage is the multiplayer mode, which makes the game even more exciting and adds a lot of fun.

developer_response.body: Thanks for appreciating our kinda whacky concept of a game :) We appreciate you taking the time and leaving this review!

reviews.review_description: I wasn't sure what to expect from this one, and was pleasantly surprised and impressed. Essentially Tertris controlled by punching a box on front of you to manipulate the placement colour-coded pieces. I got into a bit of a flow state and found it enjoyable without being taxing. Worth a try.

developer_response.body: We knew we were taking a risk with such a weird concept and mix of genres, but we are glad people are connecting to it :) Thanks for the review!

reviews.review_description: Bloxer is a brilliant and fun twist on classic puzzle games. The physical punch-based gameplay feels unique and incredibly satisfying. With multiplayer and a polished retro arcade hub, it’s an addictive and highly enjoyable VR experience. A jewel !

developer_response.body: You are too kind! Thanks for taking the time and reviewing the game, means a ton :)

reviews.review_description: Never would I've thought that a game where you punch a monitor to control an arcade game, let alone a Tetris-like, would be do much fun! One of the bigger VR surprises for me.

developer_response.body: Great to hear we positively surprised you - we couldn't hope for more! Thanks for your review :)

reviews.review_description:
Interesting combination of boxing and Tetris 
Retro graphics and multiplayer

developer_response.body: Retro is the way to go! Thanks for the review :)

reviews.review_title: Fun, and Perfect for Quick Play sessions
reviews.review_description: Bloxer is fun and easy to get into, especially if you like creative sandbox games. The visuals and performance are smooth, if you like punching things, this one is for you

developer_response.body: We LOVE punching things! Thanks for your support :)

reviews.review_description: Very fun work out with different puzzle games to enjoy!

developer_response.body: Boxing gloves was JUST what Tetris needed, especially in VR - thank you!

reviews.review_title: Great, simple idea and clean execution.
reviews.review_description: Great for tricking yourself into a few minutes exercise without realising!

developer_response.body:
Yeah, Bloxer can get REALLY intensive and arms can hurt after a longer session :D 
Thanks for the review!
